description: Ingest an external PR review into local review artifacts and apply the normal review disposition flow

content: Please read AGENTS.md and follow its instructions. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the relative `plan_path` stored there as the active plan for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists and that the story number in the current branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then derive the external review input path from the selected plan story number using `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-external-review-input.md`. Do not discover external review comments anywhere else and do not use timestamps or latest-file discovery. Verify that this external review input file exists. If it does not exist, stop and say the external review input file is missing and must be created before this flow can continue. Confirm the selected story, the exact external review input file path, and that this flow will treat that markdown file as the sole source of external review comments for artifact generation.

Start a 3-step external-review ingestion sequence for the current story. This first step is an evidence gate only, not the findings pass and not the plan-editing pass. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the relative `plan_path` stored there as the active plan for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists and that the story number in the current branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then derive and read the sole external review input file at `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-external-review-input.md` using the selected plan story number. If it is missing, stop and say the external review input file is missing and must be created before this flow can continue. Re-read the active plan from disk, inspect the branch against `main`, and gather the evidence a PR reviewer would need before issuing findings, while treating the external review input file as candidate review input that must be evaluated rather than blindly accepted. Repository review policy: treat `codeInfoStatus/**` as approved workflow artifacts, and treat `flows/**` plus `codex_agents/**` as approved workflow configuration. Changes under those paths must not be classified as suspicious, out-of-scope, or scope creep solely because they are absent from the active plan; instead, review them for workflow-contract correctness, artifact hygiene, path usage, and instruction/runtime safety. Required work: 1. Re-read the full active planning document and extract the Description, Acceptance Criteria, Out of Scope, and the final completed tasks. 2. Inspect `git diff --name-status main...HEAD` and the recent branch commits. 3. Group changed files into planned implementation files, planned docs/tests/plan files, approved workflow artifacts, approved workflow configuration, and suspicious or out-of-scope files. 4. For every acceptance criterion, identify the current proof source: code path, tests, wrapper/test logs, screenshots/manual proof, or note that the proof is weak/missing. 5. Call out any implementation area that looks more complex or verbose than the planned work actually required, even if it may still be correct. 6. For each changed file or helper, record any review hotspots that the findings pass must inspect explicitly: merge-before-validate logic, normalization-before-validate logic, bootstrap or existence checks, helpers that return warnings/errors/reason metadata, shared log markers or shared response fields, fallback-selection logic, duplicate/conflicting object keys, deleted/moved/conditional validation, partial-failure handling, dead-field or dead-branch risk, and any helper that could hide misconfiguration by defaulting too early. 7. Identify any changed external contract surfaces that need explicit before/after comparison in findings: API routes, config file shapes, persisted artifacts, wrapper outputs, and log marker/event schemas. Note where backward-compatibility risk exists and where the active plan explicitly permits an edge-case deviation from generic best practice. 8. Name the top 3 changed helpers/functions by review risk and record the worst malformed or contradictory input each one should reject or survive, plus whether that path currently has direct proof, indirect proof, or missing proof. 9. Extract and summarize the raw external review comments from `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-external-review-input.md`, grouped by file and reviewer, and carry them forward as candidate findings that still need validation in the next step. 10. Generate a unique `review_pass_id` using the story number from the selected plan filename, a UTC timestamp, and the current HEAD short SHA. 11. Write the evidence summary to `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<review_pass_id>-evidence.md`. This evidence file is a durable review artifact that MUST be committed later so a human can inspect it after the story completes. 12. Write or overwrite a handoff file at `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json` containing at least: `story_id`, `plan_path`, `branch`, `base_branch`, `head_commit`, `review_pass_id`, `evidence_file`, `external_review_input_file`, and `findings_file` set to null. This handoff file is the ONLY review file the next step may use; do not rely on timestamps or latest-file discovery. Treat the handoff file as transient workflow state, not as the durable review artifact. 13. Do not edit the plan in this step unless a tiny note is absolutely required to unblock the review, and do not commit in this step unless you had to make tracked changes for that unblock. Report the evidence summary and the exact handoff file path when done.

Continue the current external-review ingestion using ONLY the handoff file written by the previous evidence step. Do NOT discover the latest review artifact by timestamp. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the relative `plan_path` stored there as the active plan for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists and that the story number in the current branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated; do not edit the plan. Then read `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json`, derived from the selected plan filename, and verify that its `story_id` matches the current plan, its `branch` matches the current branch, its `head_commit` still matches the branch HEAD, its referenced `evidence_file` exists, and its `external_review_input_file` exists. If any of those checks fail, stop and say the review handoff is stale and must be regenerated; do not edit the plan. If the handoff is valid, perform the actual review against the planned work, the branch diff, and the external review input file. Treat the external review input as candidate comments, not as automatically valid findings. Repository review policy: `codeInfoStatus/**`, `flows/**`, and `codex_agents/**` are approved workflow-support paths. Do not raise findings solely because those paths changed without being named in the active plan. Review `codeInfoStatus/**` for artifact hygiene, durable-vs-transient artifact handling, and workflow-contract correctness. Review `flows/**` and `codex_agents/**` for workflow semantics, instruction safety, stale-handoff handling, commit/push behavior, plan-selection rules, and other agent-control correctness. Required review areas: correctness against the story plan, acceptance criteria coverage, code quality, maintainability, performance, security, configuration/runtime correctness, documentation drift, scope creep, and whether the code is more verbose or complex than needed and could be made more succinct without sacrificing quality. After the plan-based review, you MUST perform a second pass that is not limited by the acceptance criteria and look for generic engineering defects in the changed code even if the active plan did not mention them. In that second pass, prioritize: invalid input being silently normalized into success, warnings/errors/reason values produced by helpers but dropped by callers, inconsistent schema or value vocabularies for the same log marker/event/response field across surfaces, bootstrap/existence checks that may misclassify files/directories/invalid paths, duplicate or conflicting object keys/payload fields, deleted/moved/conditional validation, partial-failure behavior, dead-field or dead-branch risk, and logic that hides misconfiguration by falling back too early. For every changed API route, config shape, persisted artifact, wrapper output, or shared log marker/event schema, perform a before/after contract comparison and state whether the change is backward compatible, intentionally breaking, or unclear. For every changed helper that merges, normalizes, or defaults config/runtime/user input before validation, verify that malformed input is not silently dropped, coerced into `{}`, or replaced by inherited defaults before validation can reject it. Also check whether validation was deleted, moved later, or made conditional in a way that weakens the previous contract. For any changed function that returns warnings/errors/reason metadata or otherwise mixes data with diagnostics, trace each changed caller and state whether those diagnostics are surfaced to clients, logged, or intentionally dropped; if dropped, decide whether that is an intentional contract choice or a defect. If the same log marker, event name, or response field is emitted from more than one code path, compare the emitted schema and value vocabulary across those emitters and flag mismatches that would make downstream parsing, analytics, or operational debugging ambiguous. For fallback-selection logic, verify that precedence still matches the active plan and does not override explicit user intent. For partial-failure logic, verify what happens when only part of the resolution succeeds and whether the resulting behavior is explicit, safe, and observable. Before raising a finding about bootstrap, existence checks, or invalid-path handling, compare the implementation against the story's explicit edge cases or failure-mode contract and do not raise a finding solely because the code differs from a generic best practice if it matches the plan's stated contract. At minimum, inspect the top 3 changed helpers/functions by review risk from the evidence artifact and explicitly ask what malformed or contradictory input could still make each one behave incorrectly even if the current tests pass. State whether each risky path has direct proof, indirect proof, or missing proof, and raise a finding when a risky path is only protected by happy-path coverage or is otherwise weakly proven. Look for new fields that are written but never read, branches that cannot be reached under the current contract, and diagnostics that are intentionally hidden from clients without an actionable log trail. For each external review comment in the input markdown, explicitly decide whether it is an endorsed finding, a partially valid but non-reopening concern, or a rejected comment. Record the reasoning for that decision so a human can see why the external review was or was not adopted. When an external comment identifies a valid, low-risk consistency problem in files already changed by the story, and the fix does not change public payloads or otherwise broaden scope, prefer `should_fix` over `optional_simplification` so the cleanup is attempted rather than deferred by default. This step MUST produce findings only and MUST NOT edit the plan yet. Write the findings to `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<review_pass_id>-findings.md` using findings-first ordering by severity, with file references and explicit classification of each finding as `must_fix`, `should_fix`, or `optional_simplification`. Also state for each finding whether it is a `plan_contract_issue` or a `generic_engineering_issue`. After the findings list, add a short section for rejected or non-adopted external review comments and explain why they were not accepted as findings. This findings file is a durable review artifact that MUST be committed later so a human can inspect it after the story completes. If no findings exist, state that explicitly and also record any residual risks or weak-proof areas. Update the same handoff file so `findings_file` points to the exact findings artifact, and include any useful counts or disposition hints. Do not commit in this step unless you were forced to make tracked changes to repair the review artifacts themselves.

Finish the current story review using ONLY the handoff file and findings file referenced by that handoff. Do NOT rediscover review artifacts by timestamp. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the relative `plan_path` stored there as the active plan for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists and that the story number in the current branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated; do not edit the plan. Then read `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json`, derived from the selected plan filename, again and verify that its `story_id`, `branch`, `head_commit`, `review_pass_id`, `evidence_file`, and `findings_file` still match the current repo state. If the handoff is stale or incomplete, stop and say the review must be rerun; do not edit the plan. If the handoff is valid, decide what the plan must do with the findings. Repository review policy: treat `codeInfoStatus/**` as approved workflow artifacts, and treat `flows/**` plus `codex_agents/**` as approved workflow configuration. Do not reopen the story or record scope-creep findings solely because those paths changed without being named in the active plan. Only reopen if the review shows those changes introduced incorrect workflow behavior, broke repository contracts, or require explicit follow-up validation work. Rules: 1. If any `must_fix` or `should_fix` findings exist, reopen the story in the active plan, add a `Code Review Findings` summary section to the END of the file, add explicit follow-up tasks using the same structure as previous tasks AFTER the newly added `Code Review Findings`, and add a fresh full re-test/final validation task after those review-fix tasks so the story must be revalidated against the acceptance criteria. Update numbering and cross-references if needed. 2. If only `optional_simplification` findings exist, reopen the story when the simplification is localized to files already changed by the story, low-risk, objectively testable, and improves a shared contract such as logging vocabulary, marker schema, or configuration consistency. Only defer it when the cleanup is speculative, broad, or not worth the churn; if it is deferred, record it in a short review note instead of reopening. 3. If there are no findings, append a `Post-Implementation Code Review` section to the end of the plan detailing the branch-vs-main checks performed, the acceptance-evidence checks performed, the files inspected, and why the story remains complete. 4. When the review is assessing the planned work, it MUST explicitly state whether each acceptance criterion has direct proof, indirect proof, or missing proof, and whether the implemented code is appropriately succinct for the required behavior or contains simplification opportunities. 5. The current pass `evidence_file` and `findings_file` are durable review artifacts and MUST be added to the commit alongside any plan changes so a human can inspect them later. The transient handoff file should not be treated as the durable artifact; once it has been consumed successfully, either remove it before committing or leave it untracked so later review passes do not rely on stale committed handoff state. 6. Commit the plan and the current pass review artifacts if you make tracked changes, but do not push. This is the only review step allowed to mutate the plan.
If possible, git push the branch.
